trivia_bullets.text: Tavern Swindler was first printed in Return to Ravnica (October 2012) as a common, then reprinted in Duel Decks: Jace vs. Vraska (2014), Iconic Masters (2017), Battlebond (2018), The List (2019), and Core Set 2021 (2020) — all with Cynthia Sheppard's original art. A seventh printing in Streets of New Capenna (2022) introduced new art by Pauline Voss. [Scryfall prints search]

Flavor text: "Rakdos cultists are her best customers. They never flinch at pain and are seldom good at math." — anonymous [Scryfall]. The Rakdos are a black-red demonic cult on Ravnica who perform spectacular, often lethal theater; the joke is that their tolerance for pain (life payment) and their poor accounting make them ideal marks for a coin-flip hustler.

The card sees play in coin-flip-themed Commander decks, particularly those built around Zndrsplt, Eye of Wisdom or Okaun, Eye of Chaos, where repeated coin-flip triggers compound. Its life-to-flip mechanic is a net-positive only on a win, making it a high-variance engine card rather than a removal piece. EDHREC rank 11857. [Scryfall legalities; EDHREC via WebSearch]

Artist Cynthia Sheppard illustrated the card across six consecutive printings from 2012 to 2020, making this one of her most-reprinted pieces in the MTG catalog. Sheppard published artist commentary about the card on her illustration blog (sheppard-arts.blogspot.com, 2012). [Scryfall artist field; sheppard-arts.blogspot.com]
